title: lycopodioides microphylla
intro: Lycopodioides microphylla, also known as the ground pine or running club moss, is a species of plant in the lycopodiaceae family that grows in dense mats on the ground. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Lycopodioides microphylla has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
